messages.content:
THE TEST: Name the artifact that would SETTLE this claim — not inform it,
settle it. If a purchasable report, an existing dataset, or a week of expert
calls would settle it, the claim is an ERROR: our model of the world is wrong
today and research fixes it. If only time settles it (a market shift that
hasn't happened, a rival's response, a demand gate that may or may not bind),
it is a RISK.

Guidance:
- "The number is X, not Y" claims about TODAY's market are errors
  (market size, shares, definitions, scope mismatches, benchmark misuse).
- Claims about how the next 12–24 months UNFOLD are risks
  (substitution pace, foreclosure, construction slips, price responses).
- Mixed claims: classify by the load-bearing part. If correcting today's
  number removes most of the expected loss, it is an error.
- For an ERROR, propose the concrete correction the evidence implies
  (nodeId, corrected value, band, one-sentence rationale) and 2 targeted
  research queries (one confirming, one falsifying) with concrete entities.
- Do NOT propose corrections for risks; their perturbation already expresses
  the scenario.

THE CLAIM:
{
 "title": "Distributor channel earns nothing in Year 1, cutting the ramp",
 "narrative": "The model assumes the distributor channel sells at the same 3% pace as every other channel in Year 1. But distributors allocate category slots once a year, and a pre-launch entrant arrives mid-cycle into slots already filled by incumbent and private-label lines. So the 13% distributor cell produces no Year-1 revenue and the blended pace drops below the 3% mid-band.",
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Distributors set rack-power slots once a year; incumbents and private-label lines hold them.",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c8",
    "digest:cust-mix#c13",
    "ledger:cust.distributor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "A mid-cycle entrant waits for the next review, delaying all distribution deals past 12 months.",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "digest:cust-mix#c4",
    "ledger:cust.operator-mid"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The 13% distributor cell drops out, pulling Year-1 pace below 3%.",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.distributor",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "falsifier": "A named power or IT-infrastructure distributor signs the venture's line off-cycle within 6 months of launch, with no exclusivity conflict.",
 "category": "execution",
 "evidence": [
  {
   "title": "Mayflex signs distribution agreement with Schleifenbauer",
   "sourceType": "industry-report",
   "publisher": "Data Centre & Network News",
   "date": "2025-07-31",
   "excerpt": "Mayflex... has announced a new distribution agreement with Schleifenbauer, adding the Netherlands-based manufacturer’s power distribution units (PDUs) and energy management tools to the Elevate brand’s data centre infrastructure portfolio.",
   "url": "https://dcnnmagazine.com/infrastructure/power-cooling/mayflex-signs-distribution-agreement-with-schleifenbauer/",
   "attached": true
  },
  {
   "title": "The Ultimate Guide to a Product Line Review (PLR)",
   "sourceType": "industry-report",
   "publisher": "Porchlight",
   "date": "2025-07-10",
   "excerpt": "PLRs typically occur on a rolling calendar by category. Some happen annually, while others may occur every two to three years. In lieu of a full PLR, retailers often conduct Business Reviews.",
   "url": "https://www.porchlightatl.com/product-line-review-plr-guide/",
   "attached": true
  },
  {
   "title": "MaxPower Corporation - Line Card",
   "sourceType": "industry-report",
   "publisher": "MaxPower Corporation",
   "date": null,
   "excerpt": "MaxPower is the master distributor for GE, Mitsubishi, Staco, Toshiba, Xtreme Power Conversion... Rackmount Power Distribution Units... XPDUs feature rich, network-grade PDU's at half the price of comparable models.",
   "url": "https://maxpowercorp.com/line_card.html",
   "attached": true
  }
 ]
}

THE LEDGER FACTS IT TARGETS (current values, derivations, bands):
[
 {
  "id": "cust.distributor",
  "label": "Distributor",
  "kind": "estimated",
  "value": 0.13,
  "unit": "ratio",
  "confidence": "inferred",
  "maturity": "needs-source",
  "dimension": "customerType",
  "dimensionValue": "distributor",
  "sensitivityRange": null,
  "funnelActive": true,
  "derivation": null,
  "provenance": {
   "rationale": "Master-distributor models exist (documented qualitatively) but indirect share is unquantified → internal estimate.",
   "promotionPath": "Validate buyer mix with 3 CE operator/distributor interviews."
  },
  "evidence": []
 },
 {
  "id": "obtainableFactor",
  "label": "Year-1 obtainable share of SAM",
  "kind": "assumption",
  "value": 0.03,
  "unit": "ratio",
  "confidence": "inferred",
  "maturity": "triangulated",
  "dimension": null,
  "dimensionValue": null,
  "sensitivityRange": {
   "low": 0.01,
   "high": 0.05
  },
  "funnelActive": true,
  "derivation": {
   "method": "Benchmark anchoring",
   "expression": "Year-1 SOM benchmarks: 1% of SAM (Prospeo); early-stage range 1–5% (IdeaPlan) → curated 3% mid-band",
   "crossCheck": "Prior 6% assumption sat above every year-1 benchmark found — revised down"
  },
  "provenance": {
   "rationale": "Two independent benchmark sources agree on 1–5%, but both are generic (not DC-hardware-specific) → inferred.",
   "promotionPath": "Replace with pipeline-based model once first CE deals close (win-rate × cycle time × capacity)."
  },
  "evidence": [
   {
    "title": "Addressable markets — SOM benchmarks",
    "sourceType": "analyst-estimate",
    "publisher": "Prospeo",
    "date": null,
    "excerpt": "SOM benchmarks that won't get you laughed out of the room: 1% of SAM in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 5% in Year 3.",
    "attached": true
   },
   {
    "title": "Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM) — early-stage ranges",
    "sourceType": "analyst-estimate",
    "publisher": "IdeaPlan",
    "date": "2024",
    "excerpt": "For early-stage companies, SOM is typically 1-5% of SAM. For growing companies with product-market fit, SOM can reach 10-20% of SAM.",
    "attached": true
   }
  ]
 }
]

Emit JSON: { resolution: "error"|"risk", settleTest, proposedCorrection: {nodeId, value, low, high, rationale} or null, researchQueries: [{query, kind}] ([] for risks) }

content: {"resolution":"risk","settleTest":"Only the passage of Year 1 settles whether the distributor channel books zero revenue. Whether a specific entrant lands an off-cycle distribution deal within 6 months (as the falsifier states) depends on future distributor decisions and negotiation outcomes that have not yet happened — no purchasable report or dataset can resolve today whether this particular venture's line gets slotted in-cycle or off-cycle.","proposedCorrection":null,"researchQueries":[]}
